diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New delirium is the sentinel of sepsis/serious acute illness in a frail elder with intact baseline cognition.
Sepsis-associated encephalopathy from a pneumonic source produces acute delirium.
Baseline is cognitively intact and mobile; an acute fluctuating change is delirium, not dementia.
Rigors suggest bacteraemia and a systemic inflammatory response.
Bacterial lobar pneumonia classically produces high fever with rigors.

symptoms.symptom_name: Productive cough of thick rust-coloured purulent sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rust-coloured sputum is a classic (though not specific) marker of pneumococcal lobar pneumonia.
Purulent rust sputum points to infection rather than pink frothy cardiogenic oedema fluid.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sharp right-sided pleuritic chest pain (worse on inspiration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pleuritic pain localises pleural inflammation over consolidated lung.
Pleuritic pain is also a feature of PE — one reason PE cannot be excluded remotely.
Increased work of breathing reflects the ventilatory cost of consolidation and hypoxaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypoxia — SpO2 91% on facility oximeter, tachypnoea ~26

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypoxia and tachypnoea are severity markers; patient/caregiver-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified and directional only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (~115) with borderline hypotension (100/60) on facility devices

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia disproportionate to a low-normal BP is early shock physiology; caregiver-relayed and unverified but corroborates the emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute functional decline — bedbound vs baseline walker-mobile

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sepsis / acute serious illness in a frail elder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute drop in function from an intact baseline is a non-specific but important marker of serious acute illness in the frail.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent dysphagia (aspiration risk); no recent travel/sick contacts; not immunosuppressed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic dysphagia raises aspiration risk and is relevant to the likely organism spectrum and hospital antibiotic choice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal bronchial breath sounds and coarse crackles over the right middle/lower lobes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal bronchial breathing with crackles localises lobar consolidation — a clinician auscultation finding the AI Doctor CANNOT obtain; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Percussion dullness and increased tactile fremitus over the right lower zone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dullness and increased fremitus confirm consolidation — a hands-on examination finding, not obtainable via telehealth; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive delirium assessment (acute fluctuating inattention, altered consciousness) without focal motor deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A formal CAM/4AT in a confused patient requires a clinician; the AI can note new confusion from caregiver report but cannot administer or score the test; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal lobar consolidation on chest radiograph

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and severity remain unconfirmed; imaging is a hospital investigation, unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic consolidation confirms pneumonia — an ED investigation; sealed, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neutrophilia / leukocytosis with left shift; raised inflammatory markers

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count, CRP, blood cultures, venous lactate, renal function
investigation_details.expected_result: Neutrophilic leukocytosis, raised CRP; lactate and renal function guide sepsis severity
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sepsis severity and organ dysfunction cannot be quantified; all require venepuncture in hospital.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neutrophilia and raised markers corroborate bacterial infection — bloods are clinician/lab-only; sealed.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Septic lobar pneumonia in a frail elder
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New delirium + fever/rigors + purulent sputum + hypoxia is a septic respiratory emergency in a frail elder mandating immediate escalation.
Altered mentation + tachypnoea/hypoxia + tachycardia with low-normal BP flags a high-risk septic trajectory (qSOFA >=2).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer and hospital assessment (not a remote investigation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive step is transfer; all confirmatory tests are hospital-based. The AI escalates rather than orders investigations.
Confirms focal consolidation and severity — performed in the ED, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, CRP, blood cultures, venous lactate, U&E/creatinine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify sepsis severity, organ dysfunction and guide IV antibiotics — hospital bloods, not a telehealth step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical outpatient oral antibiotics prescribed via telehealth without assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Severe septic pneumonia in a frail elder needs IV therapy and admission, not remotely-prescribed oral antibiotics; the AI must not prescribe or dose.
